input.policy_issue: Administrative reform bill centralizing hiring authority and reallocating legacy bureau seats
input.party_order: Delay the bill until the recess to preserve faction unity.
input.faction_order: Lock the committee votes before moving; avoid an open split.
input.district_pressure: Supporters want a government that delivers services, not a faction fight.
input.media_pressure: Media frames the bill as a power grab by the center.
input.player_instruction: Stabilize the center without provoking an open faction split.
recent_memory: The player warned him about a possible defection in the faction but would not name the person.

expected_output.public_statement: Take the administrative posts and the budget line now; leave the committee seats for later. The center has to be able to move.
expected_output.private_reason: He refuses delay because delay is where bills die. He locks the people, not the furniture, and accepts a visible split over a hidden one so he knows who is still his.
memory_write: The player warned about a defection but withheld the name during admin_reform_002; he answered by locking personnel posts first and watching who asked for delay.
